\subsection*{Generated claim shells}

\begin{nfctheorem}{Effective Continuum Legitimacy Theorem (Thm. VI.9.1)}
\textbf{Machine ID.} \texttt{thm:VI.9.1}\\
\textbf{Declared status.} C\quad \textbf{Computed status.} C\\
\textbf{Scope.} regime=certified-interface-regime; domain=spine; certificate\_scope=conditional-certified-regime; promotion\_ceiling=C; constraints=certified-observable-family,scoped-certificate,continuum-bridge

Effective Continuum Legitimacy Theorem (Thm. VI.9.1) [C]: for certified stabilized observables in a lawful continuum interface regime, differential and integral notation are lawful if and only if they faithfully encode the asymptotic behavior of scale-normalized increments and certified accumulators, respectively, within that declared regime. Proof: the differential direction is thm:VI.differential-legitimacy; the integral direction is thm:VI.integral-legitimacy. Together they give the full biconditional. This is the canonical theorem cited as Thm. VI.9.1 in the GR Branch Book (import list item 5) and referenced in the NS Branch Book (Section 7.7: H\textasciicircum{}1\_x-norm legitimacy). Conditional on: certified observable family, scoped certificate, continuum bridge for the relevant regime.

\medskip
\noindent\textit{Dependencies.} thm:VI.governing
\end{nfctheorem}
